mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary policy stance that reduces corporate discount rates and credit spreads, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank correlation between monthly FEDFUNDS changes and 20-day forward returns is below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or triggered instances do not show positive mean returns (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the unemployment rate of more than 0.3 percentage points over a three-month window signals deteriorating labor income and consumer spending power, which reduces expected corporate revenues and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances when UNRATE has risen more than 0.3 percentage points over the prior 3 months during any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent month-over-month decline in real retail sales and industrial production indicates a coordinated contraction in consumer and manufacturing demand that amplifies recession probability, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and INDPRO both decline month-over-month
failure_condition: The interaction term of contemporaneous RSAFS and INDPRO monthly changes fails to achieve a Rank IC above 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or the average 60-day return following joint declines is not negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is below 1%, an acceleration in consumer price inflation erodes real corporate margins and reduces the present value of future cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: In periods where FEDFUNDS is below 1%, month-over-month increases in CPIAUCSL are followed by positive average 5-day S&P 500 returns over a 2-year rolling window (t-test p < 0.05 for positive mean).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly housing starts reading below 550 thousand units signals a freeze in residential construction activity and associated durable goods demand, which lowers expected aggregate output and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 55% of months when HOUST is below 550 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing output and rising capacity utilization, which supports corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year Spearman correlation between month-over-month INDPRO changes and 60-day forward returns falls below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in nonfarm payrolls while the unemployment rate exceeds 8% indicates a distressed labor market where rising joblessness suppresses aggregate income and triggers equity risk-off, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition of falling PAYEMS and UNRATE above 8% fails to produce negative average 20-day S&P 500 returns over a 3-year rolling window (t-test p > 0.05 for negative mean).

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth below 1% annualized signals near-stagnant aggregate demand and disincentivizes capital expenditure, which causes broad-based de-risking and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.05) following quarters with GDPC1 year-over-year growth below 1%.
